mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly AI interface that supports various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It is designed for self-hosting and can operate entirely offline, making it suitable for users who require a customizable AI deployment solution. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and potential complexity in setup compared to managed solutions.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Open WebUI Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the application easily.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

```

> This command adds the Open WebUI Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Open WebUI to function properly, as they define the custom resources used by the application.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/v0.8.8/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
This command installs Open WebUI using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --namespace open-webui --create-namespace --version 0.8.8

```

> This installs Open WebUI in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Create Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
Creating an example custom resource allows you to test the functionality of Open WebUI.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: openwebui.io/v1
kind: WebUI
metadata:
  name: example-webui
spec:
  description: Example Open WebUI instance
EOF

```

> This command creates an example instance of Open WebUI.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: openwebui.io/v1
kind: WebUI
metadata:
  name: example-webui
spec:
  description: Example Open WebUI instance
EOF

steps.title: Verify Custom Resource is Ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the custom resource to ensure that Open WebUI is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get webui example-webui -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the status of the example WebUI instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
This step removes Open WebUI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the application.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls Open WebUI from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Open WebUI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get webui example-webui -o yaml > backup-example-webui.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --namespace open-webui --version 0.8.8

kubectl get webui example-webui -o yaml

```

> Backup the current custom resource.
> This command upgrades Open WebUI to the specified version.
> Verify that the custom resource is still functioning after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get webui example-webui -o yaml > backup-example-webui.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'webui.openwebui.io not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The custom resource definition may not have been applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/v0.8.8/deploy/crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to OpenAI API'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenAI API URL may not be configured correctly in the custom resource.

**Fix:**
Check and update the OpenAI API URL in your custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using: kubectl logs <pod-name> and ensure all required configurations are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated to run Open WebUI.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests/limits in the deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration features, and enhanced user permissions management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: When everything is working, you should see the output of 'kubectl get webui example-webui' showing the status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: openwebui.io/v1
kind: WebUI
metadata:
  name: example-webui
spec:
  description: Example Open WebUI instance
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
